chief_complaint: 51-year-old Syrian male presenting with inferior ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction.
history: Coronary artery anomalies represent morphological deviations from standard coronary anatomy, occurring in less than 1% of the general population. The patient had an anomalous aortic origin of a coronary artery arising from the ascending aorta.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: ECG: inferior ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction; Bloods: not provided; Imaging: not provided.
correct_diagnosis: Anomalous aortic origin of a coronary artery arising from the ascending aorta causing inferior ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction.
differentials: Anomalous aortic origin of a coronary artery arising from the ascending aorta causing inferior ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction.

Acute myocardial infarction due to coronary artery disease
